data.title: Borr Drilling reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Borr Drilling press release (NYSE:BORR): Q4 revenue of $263.1M (+19.3% Y/Y) beats by $15.32M.
  • Fourth Quarter 2024 net income of $26.3 million. 
  • Fourth Quarter 2024 Adjusted EBITDA of $136.7 million.

data.title: ProMIS Neurosciences dismisses COO position
data.description:
• ProMIS Neurosciences (NASDAQ:PMN) said on February 14, 2025, it separated with Gavin T. Malenfant as Chief Operating Officer of the company.
  • The company also terminated the Chief Operating Officer position, and does not intend to replace Malenfant.

data.title: Klaviyo files to sell common stocks
data.description:
• Klaviyo (NYSE:KVYO) filed to sell Series A common stocks, no amount given.
  • Filing

data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles rose 3.3M barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a build of 3.3M barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending February 14.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a build of 2.8M barrels for the week, distillate inventories show a draw of 2.7M barrels, and stockpiles at the Cushing, Oklahoma, storage hub show a build of 1.7M barrels.

The Energy Information Administration will release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report on Thursday; analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecast domestic commercial crude stocks will increase by 2.4M barrels, gasoline inventories are expected to remain unchanged, and distillate inventories will decline by 1.7M barrels.

WTI April crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $72.25/bbl after settling Wednesday at $72.10/bbl.

ETF: (NYSEARCA:USO)

More on crude oil

data.title: The Hershey Company announces pricing of notes
data.description:
• The Hershey Company (NYSE:HSY) announced the pricing of its offering of $500M of 4.550% notes due 2028, $500M of 4.750% notes due 2030, $500M of 4.950% notes due 2032 and $500M of 5.100% notes due 2035 in a public offering.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds of the Notes Offering to repay all of its outstanding 0.900% Senior Notes due 2025 and 3.200% Senior Notes due 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ALUULA Composites announces share consolidation
data.description:
• ALUULA Composites (TSXV:AUUA:CA) on Wednesday said it intends to consolidate its issued and outstanding common shares on the basis of one post- consolidation share for every 20 pre-consolidation shares of the company.
  • Based on the current number of issued and outstanding shares, the consolidation is expected to reduce the number of issued and outstanding shares from 501.23M shares, to about 25.06M shares.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Nutrien sees strong crop input demand in 2025
Gold eases off record highs as dollar holds ground
data.description:
Gold futures fell slightly on Wednesday after giving up early gains, as the dollar ticked higher against rival currencies, making gold more expensive for other currency holders.

The breadth and uncertainty of U.S. tariffs have increased overall market volatility, which is beneficial for gold, and increased U.S. prices compared to other parts of the world, Sprott's Ryan McIntyre wrote.

A Russia-Ukraine war resolution could moderate gold's performance in the short term, but such a deal would not resolve much of the wider geopolitical and economic uncertainty, McIntyre said, adding that gold should continue its strong, sustained performance and reach new highs in the coming years.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for February delivery finished -0.4% to $2,919.40/oz, still the front month's third highest close in history, and front-month February silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -0.9% to $32.993/oz, its second highest settlement value YTD.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

The physical gold market is experiencing unprecedented stress, with massive outflows from London to U.S. vaults and an increasing number of countries repatriating their gold reserves, Andy Schectman, President of Miles Franklin Precious Metals, told Kitco.

Schectman points to data showing that more than 12.5M oz of gold and 40M oz of silver have moved from London into the U.S. Comex system since last November, emphasizing that the sheer scale of the transfers suggests a deeper shift in global monetary policy.

The analyst also noted that J.P. Morgan is set to deliver $4B worth of gold into Comex for the February contract, questioning which entities are acquiring such vast quantities of gold: "What customer's got $4 billion in their back pocket to buygold with?"
